title: Badge is awarded when a student reaches a defined academic milestone
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-20
A badge milestone is configured: all homework in course-2000 submitted on time
student-20 has submitted all homework for course-2000 on time
steps: Trigger the milestone evaluation for student-20 on course-2000
Query the database for badge records belonging to student-20
expected_result: The database contains a badge record for student-20 linked to the on-time-homework milestone for course-2000 with an awarded status.

title: Badge is not awarded when the academic milestone has not been reached
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-21
A badge milestone is configured: all homework in course-2001 submitted on time
student-21 has one late submission in course-2001
steps: Trigger the milestone evaluation for student-21 on course-2001
Query the database for badge records belonging to student-21 for this milestone
expected_result: No badge record is created for student-21 for the on-time-homework milestone on course-2001.

title: Badge is not awarded a second time when the same milestone is reached again
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-22
student-22 already has a badge awarded for the on-time-homework milestone on course-2002
steps: Trigger the milestone evaluation for student-22 on course-2002 a second time
Query the database for badge records belonging to student-22 for this milestone
expected_result: Exactly one badge record exists for student-22 for the on-time-homework milestone on course-2002. No duplicate is created.

title: Grade-improvement badge carries a positive numeric reward value
steps: Authenticate as the admin
Send POST /admin/badges with body: type=grade_improvement, reward_value=5, milestone_id=milestone-1
Query the database for the created badge definition
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 201. The database record for the badge has type grade_improvement and reward_value equal to 5.

title: Grade-improvement badge with zero reward value is rejected
steps: Authenticate as the admin
Send POST /admin/badges with body: type=grade_improvement, reward_value=0, milestone_id=milestone-2
Capture the HTTP response status
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 422. No badge definition record is created in the database.

title: Grade-improvement badge reward value greater than one hundred points is rejected
steps: Authenticate as the admin
Send POST /admin/badges with body: type=grade_improvement, reward_value=101, milestone_id=milestone-3
Capture the HTTP response status
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 422. No badge definition record is created in the database.

title: Late-penalty waiver badge carries a waiver entitlement flag
steps: Authenticate as the admin
Send POST /admin/badges with body: type=late_penalty_waiver, milestone_id=milestone-4
Query the database for the created badge definition
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 201. The database record for the badge has type late_penalty_waiver and a waiver_entitlement field set to true.

title: Late-penalty waiver badge cannot be created with a grade_improvement reward_value field
steps: Authenticate as the admin
Send POST /admin/badges with body: type=late_penalty_waiver, reward_value=5, milestone_id=milestone-5
Capture the HTTP response status
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 422 indicating that a late-penalty waiver badge must not carry a grade improvement reward value. No badge definition is created.

title: Grade-improvement badge and late-penalty waiver badge are stored as distinct types
preconditions: A grade_improvement badge with id badge-10 exists in the database
A late_penalty_waiver badge with id badge-11 exists in the database
steps: Authenticate as the admin
Send GET /admin/badges/badge-10 and capture the type field
Send GET /admin/badges/badge-11 and capture the type field
expected_result: badge-10 returns type grade_improvement with a numeric reward_value. badge-11 returns type late_penalty_waiver with a waiver_entitlement of true and no reward_value field. The two types are distinct and non-overlapping.
